content.boundary: Applies to the counterfactual trajectory of the current legal and administrative status quo (Act CXC of 2011 unchanged, admissions mechanism unchanged, capacity policy unchanged) under continued demographic decline; excludes any new ministerial, legislative, or funding intervention.
content.change_lever: None (status quo policy baseline); the only 'lever' is the passive, demographically-mechanical effect of shrinking cohorts interacting with existing legal class-size minimums and existing capacity/geography patterns.
content.name: Demographically-driven passive contraction baseline (no new policy)
content.system_problem: Hungary's accelerating birth-cohort decline (record-low 77,500 births in 2024, 72,000 projected 2025, TFR at 1.31) combined with statutory minimum class-size rules (26-34 students) creates a mechanical, policy-independent constraint on the viability of multi-track selective provision, particularly in rural and low-density regions, even absent any deliberate equity or structural intervention.
